scope: Every dispatch record carries honest economics — real cost computed from the CONFIRMED model's pricing or carried from the CLI's own report, an explicit cost_source, true token split, duration, and confirmed model-id — so a subscription dispatch is never recorded as fake $0 API spend, and no cost figure is ever guessed or fabricated.

test_results.output: empty — all 24 changed .go files already gofmt-clean

test_results.command: go test -count=1 ./internal/model/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/state/... ./internal/run/... ./internal/driver/... ./internal/verify/... ./internal/verdict/... ./internal/captain/... (AC-05's named command plus the additional touched packages design.md's traceability section identifies)
go test -count=1 -timeout 300s ./... (full suite, fresh cache — 45 packages ok, 0 FAIL, zero regressions in any untouched package)

reachability.evidence: go test -count=1 -v -run TestRunSlice_CostSourceThreadedToStatusJSON ./internal/run/ — PASS. Drives the real RunSlice engine entry point (the same function cmd/sworn's implementer/verifier loop calls), not a leaf state.Dispatch struct literal: a fake driver's implementer leg reports CostSource=pricing-table and its verifier leg reports CostSource=cli, RunSlice runs the full implement -> verify -> transition-to-verified path, and the test reads the WRITTEN status.json off disk afterward, asserting verification.dispatches[].cost_source carries each role's distinct value through unmodified, and that the resulting file still validates against slice-status-v1 (baton.ValidateSchema) — proving the additive omitempty field is schema-safe end to end, not just by inspection of R-02's stated mitigation. Two supporting non-leaf-adjacent proofs: TestClaudeEnvelopeExplicitZeroCostIsUnknown (internal/driver/claude_test.go) drives ClaudeDriver.Dispatch with a real fake-CLI subprocess spawn and an envelope carrying an EXPLICIT reported zero cost, proving the D1 ruling end to end through the actual subprocess spawn/parse path, not a costSource()-in-isolation unit test; TestInprocessImplementerPricingTable (internal/driver/inprocess/inprocess_test.go) drives InProcess.Dispatch against a real httptest server returning a priced model ID, proving AC-02's happy path computes CostUSD from the true accumulated token split via the pricing registry, through the actual chatMeter/agent.Run loop.

delivered.item: AC-01: internal/model/pricing.go (Pricing map + ComputeCost) deleted; anthropic.go/oai.go/openai_responses.go redirected to the surviving PriceForModel/ComputeCostFromTokens registry; internal/agent/agent.go's flat-rate computeCost deleted along with the float64 cost return from Run's signature (both call sites already discarded it). TestPricingUnified enumerates every model key across modelPricing/anthropicPricing/googlePricing/bedrockPricing and asserts PriceForModel resolves each to the exact source-map rate.
delivered.evidence: internal/model/pricing_test.go:TestPricingUnified; internal/model/oai.go, anthropic.go, openai_responses.go (redirected call sites); internal/agent/agent.go (Run's new 3-return signature)

delivered.item: AC-02: InProcess.economics() computes CostUSD from the CONFIRMED response model-id (meter.modelID, not the requested string) and the true accumulated token split via the unified pricing registry, with CostSource=pricing-table on a hit and CostSource=unknown + a stderr warning naming the model on a miss (AC-04). The CostSource=provider vocabulary exists as a named constant (driver.CostSourceProviderReported) with the pre-existing amended AC-02 spec text as its contract; no live dispatch path emits it — this session added no new code for that branch, matching the amendment.

AC-03: claude.go's costSource() classifies a strictly-positive TotalCostUSD as CostSourceCLI; nil and an explicit reported zero BOTH classify CostSourceUnknown (D1 ruling — no TotalCostUSD==0->subscription inference implemented). codex.go's costSource() always returns CostSourceUnknown (D2 ruling — no Usage!=nil->subscription inference implemented; codexEnvelope never carries a cost field in any documented shape). No CLI path ever fabricates an API-equivalent price for a subscription dispatch.
delivered.evidence: internal/driver/claude.go:costSource(); internal/driver/claude_test.go:TestClaudeDispatchImplementer (positive->cli) and TestClaudeEnvelopeExplicitZeroCostIsUnknown (explicit zero->unknown, D1); internal/driver/codex.go:costSource(); internal/driver/codex_test.go:TestCodexDispatchImplementer (always unknown, D2)

delivered.item: AC-04: an unrecognised confirmed model-id records CostUSD=0 with CostSource=unknown and a stderr warning naming the model — no guessed or defaulted rate, exercised by the same code path AC-02's fail-closed branch covers (no new code needed, per spec.json's explicit posture: no pricing DATA added for models absent from every table).

AC-05: state.Dispatch and verdict.Result both carry cost_source (additive, omitempty); verify.go's withDispatchEconomics and acceptStructuredVerdict copy it off driver.Result; all 5 state.Dispatch{} construction sites in run/slice.go thread it (captain, implementer, verifier carry their real source; the two synthetic zero-cost sites — proof-absent, first-pass — correctly leave it empty, honestly, since no driver call was made). go test ./internal/model/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/state/... ./internal/run/... ./internal/driver/... ./internal/verify/... ./internal/verdict/... ./internal/captain/... passes.

D3: named driver.CostSource* constants (ProviderReported/PricingTable/CLI/Subscription/Unknown) mirroring the existing ErrKind* pattern, in internal/driver/driver.go — every producer and every test now references these constants, not a scattered string literal.
delivered.evidence: internal/driver/driver.go (CostSource* const block); grep across internal/driver, internal/model, internal/run, internal/verify, internal/captain confirms zero remaining bare CostSource string literals in production code

delivered.item: D1/D2 (Coach-ratified Type-1, status.json design_decisions): recorded before code was written, per Rule 9. Both rulings are the STRICTER of the two options design.md itself proposed — ship CostSourceUnknown universally rather than implement either subscription inference — because no positively identified, testable marker exists in the currently observed claude-cli/codex output for either.

not_delivered: D1's claude.go TotalCostUSD==0 -> CostSourceSubscription inference — NOT implemented (Rule 2: why = no positively identified, testable marker in the currently observed claude-cli output distinguishes an explicit-zero-because-subscription from an explicit-zero-because-genuinely-free-turn-or-envelope-quirk; tracking = status.json design_decisions.D1 + this proof.json entry; acknowledgement = Coach-ratified fail-closed in captain-proceed.md@4753eb3 pin 1, restated in this session's task brief). Ships CostSourceUnknown instead for the ambiguous-zero case.

D2's codex.go Usage!=nil -> CostSourceSubscription inference — NOT implemented (Rule 2: why = Usage!=nil only proves a turn completed, not that it was subscription-covered rather than API-billed, and codexEnvelope carries no field distinguishing the two; tracking = status.json design_decisions.D2 + this proof.json entry; acknowledgement = Coach-ratified fail-closed in captain-proceed.md@4753eb3 pin 1, restated in this session's task brief). Ships CostSourceUnknown universally for codex.go instead.

sworn#89 (Google/Bedrock pricing-lookup duplication into the unified registry) — out of scope, filed and acknowledged at design review (review.md pin 3: all three Rule 2 legs present). Carried forward unchanged this session; no new work against it.
divergence: Touchpoints expanded beyond spec.json's original list — design.md's grounding section found the literal pricing lookup happens in internal/model/oai.go and internal/model/anthropic.go (not named in spec.json's touchpoints), and AC-03's honesty claim required internal/driver/claude.go, claude_test.go, codex.go, codex_test.go, subprocess_test.go plus internal/verdict/verdict.go and internal/captain/review_test.go / internal/verify/verify_agentic_test.go for the cost_source field's full AC-05 thread. All were flagged in design.md's 'Files to touch' table before code (superseding spec.json's touchpoints per the design's own grounding), reviewed at design review, and are additive/same-signature redirects except agent.Run's signature drop (captain-proceed.md pin 4, blast radius independently reconfirmed contained to inprocess.go:183 and inprocess_verify.go:43, both already discarding the value).

D1/D2's shipped behaviour (CostSourceUnknown universally) is the OPPOSITE of design.md's own 'Approach per acceptance criterion' section, which proposed implementing both subscription inferences as a design pin flagged for Captain/Coach ratification. The Coach's ratification (captain-proceed.md@4753eb3 pin 1, restated verbatim in this session's task brief) overrode the design's proposal in favour of the stricter fail-closed posture — this is the correct, expected outcome of the design_review gate (Rule 9: a design's proposal is not binding until ratified), not an implementer-session deviation from an already-acknowledged design.

Test command expanded beyond AC-05's literal named string (go test ./internal/model/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/state/... ./internal/run/...) to also include ./internal/driver/... ./internal/verify/... ./internal/verdict/... ./internal/captain/... — design.md's own AC-traceability section identified these as the additional touched packages beyond spec.json's list; confirmed necessary since AC-02/AC-03/D3 land entirely inside internal/driver and internal/verify/verdict/captain carry the cost_source thread.
